title: BofA: The e-commerce surge continued in July, early data constructive for Amazon Investing.com -- BofA said on Tuesday that the e-commerce growth trend remained strong in July, with aggregated credit and debit card data showing total online spending rose 7% year over year, unchanged from June.

BofA: Intel’s new approach may ease execution pressure Investing.com -- Bank of America told investors on Tuesday that it views Intel’s recent shift in strategy as a “credit positive,” highlighting a more sensible foundry approach focused on “greater scrutiny” of customer alignment and returns.

Should you buy the pullback in Russell 2000? Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Tuesday, Bank of America urged caution on small-cap stocks despite recent pullbacks, citing valuation concerns and ongoing risks for the Russell 2000.

Wall Street Firms Complete First 24/7 U.S. Treasury Financing Via Tokenization on Canton Network The transaction used the USDC stablecoin and tokenized Treasuries for instant weekend settlement on Digital Asset's privacy-focused blockchain.

Bank of America Lowers US Rates Outlook on Weak Data, Fed Risks Strategists led by Mark Cabana cut their year-end forecast for two-year yields to 3.5%, from 3.75% previously.  “Recent US data has meaningfully shifted market Fed pricing and our view on US rates,” Cabana wrote in a note Monday.  While Bank of America’s economics team still expects the Fed to remain on hold until the second half of 2026, the rate strategists say soft labor market data has increased downside risks to interest rates.
